page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, supported by 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. The CPU includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI) and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA extensions.

For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, which provides high-speed local data access. Network capabilities are characterized by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps, making it highly optimized for data-intensive network transfers and EBS-backed storage operations.

Benchmark evaluations place the m6idn.8xlarge in the average performance tier for most compute and memory workloads. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 30,911.76 and a PassMark CPU score of 35,392.93. Memory performance is solid, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 146.7 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 67.89 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, achieving 260.68 tokens/sec with a 135M model, though performance drops to a weak 6.05 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring a blend of high-speed local storage, substantial memory, and high network throughput without the premium cost of specialized GPU or memory-intensive instances. The m6idn.8xlarge is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance across standard compute and memory tests, with a PassMark CPU score of 35,392.93 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 30,911.76. It demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 260.68 tokens/sec, but lacks GPU acceleration for larger models. This balanced hardware profile is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring fast local storage and high network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and 50 Gbps baseline networking.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
Bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance storage
High-speed 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Strong performance in small-model LLM text generation at 260.68 tokens/sec
Best suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation
